title: Haworthia atrovirens
taxonomy: Haworthia atrovirens is a species of succulent plant in the genus Haworthia. It belongs to the family Asphodelaceae and is native to South Africa. The classification of Haworthia atrovirens includes the kingdom Plantae, followed by the clade Angiosperms, the clade Monocots, the order Asparagales, and the family Asphodelaceae. Within the Asphodelaceae family, Haworthia atrovirens is classified as part of the subfamily Asphodeloideae. This succulent plant is further classified into the genus Haworthia and the species H. atrovirens.

intro: Haworthia atrovirens is a species of succulent plant within the genus Haworthia, characterized by its dark green, pointed leaves and compact growth habit. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may aid in wound healing, reducing inflammation, and fighting infections. The bioactive constituents of Haworthia atrovirens include flavonoids, saponins, and other compounds that contribute to its medicinal properties.
